meta.title: Sameba Cathedral – Holy Trinity Cathedral of Tbilisi
meta.description: Complete visitor guide to Sameba Cathedral (Holy Trinity Cathedral), the largest cathedral in Georgia. Hours, photos, reviews, and travel tips.

hero.title: თბილისის სამების საკათედრო ტაძარი
hero.subtitle: Holy Trinity Cathedral of Tbilisi
hero.tagline: The largest cathedral in Georgia and one of the largest Orthodox cathedrals in the world

about.title: About
about.description: Sameba Cathedral (Holy Trinity Cathedral) stands on Elia Hill in Tbilisi, Georgia. Completed in 2004, it is the main cathedral of the Georgian Orthodox Church and the third-tallest Eastern Orthodox cathedral in the world, reaching 84 meters in height.
highlights: Largest cathedral in the South Caucasus region
Gold-domed masterpiece visible across Tbilisi's skyline
Houses religious relics and stunning frescoes
Built with traditional Georgian architectural elements
Free entry – open to all visitors
Peaceful gardens and panoramic city views

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Discover Sameba Cathedral through photos
captions: The golden dome of Sameba Cathedral at sunset
Grand interior with ornate frescoes and iconography
Panoramic view from the cathedral courtyard
Stone facade and traditional Georgian architectural details
The bell tower and surrounding gardens
Interior candlelight creating a spiritual atmosphere
Aerial view showing the cathedral complex

reviews.title: Real Visitor Reviews
reviews.declaration: Ratings and reviews sourced from Google Maps (last updated: 2026). We only display a curated selection of verified high-rated reviews. To see all complete and latest reviews, click the link below.
reviews.moreReviews: See all reviews on Google Maps

reviewsList.name: Nino K.
reviewsList.text: Absolutely breathtaking cathedral. The gold dome gleaming against the blue sky is a sight you'll never forget. The interior is equally impressive with beautiful frescoes.

reviewsList.name: Alexander M.
reviewsList.text: One of the most impressive religious buildings I've ever visited. The sheer scale is awe-inspiring. Don't miss the panoramic views of Tbilisi from the courtyard.

reviewsList.name: Sarah T.
reviewsList.text: A must-visit in Tbilisi. The cathedral is magnificent both inside and out. Very peaceful atmosphere. Remember to dress modestly – scarves are available at the entrance.

reviewsList.name: Giorgi B.
reviewsList.text: Our national treasure. The architecture blends modern and traditional Georgian styles beautifully. Best visited early morning when the light through the windows is magical.

reviewsList.name: Maria P.
reviewsList.text: Stunning cathedral with impressive grounds. It can get crowded during services. I recommend visiting on a weekday morning for a quieter experience.

reviewsList.name: Kenji W.
reviewsList.text: The scale of this cathedral is truly remarkable. Beautiful gardens surround it, and the views over the city are fantastic. A highlight of our Georgia trip.

reviewsList.name: Elena V.
reviewsList.text: Visited during evening service – the chanting echoing through the massive interior was an unforgettable spiritual experience. The golden dome lit up at night is spectacular.

reviewsList.name: David L.
reviewsList.text: Impressive modern cathedral. The underground levels with the baptistery are worth exploring. Free admission makes it even better. Allow at least 1-2 hours for the full visit.

dressCode.title: Dress Code
items: Women should cover their heads (scarves available at entrance)
Shoulders and knees must be covered
Modest, respectful attire required
Wraps provided free at the entrance if needed

gettingThere.title: Getting There
items: Metro: Avlabari station (5-min walk)
Bus: Routes 31, 44, 55 stop nearby
Taxi: ~5 GEL from central Tbilisi
Walking: 15 min from the Old Town

tips.title: Tips
items: Visit early morning for fewer crowds and best light
Photography allowed outside; limited inside during services
Explore the underground levels and baptistery
Combine with a walk through Old Town and Rike Park
Allow 1-2 hours for a complete visit
Night visit recommended for illuminated exterior views

nearby.title: Nearby Attractions
items: Rike Park & Bridge of Peace – 10 min walk
Narikala Fortress – 20 min walk
Old Town (Abanotubani) – 15 min walk
Metekhi Church – 10 min walk
Tbilisi Botanical Garden – 25 min walk

sources.title: Data & References
sources.text: This is an independent third-party tourism information site providing objective visitor guidance. Information is sourced from public resources (Georgian National Tourism Administration, Wikipedia, and visitor contributions) combined with Google Maps data.
items: Google Maps – ratings, reviews, and location data
Unsplash – photography
Georgian National Tourism Administration
Wikipedia – historical information

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This privacy policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your information when you visit our website.

sections.title: Information We Collect
sections.content: We collect minimal data: basic analytics (page views, browser type, country), language preferences stored locally, and theme preferences stored locally. We do not collect personal information unless you contact us directly.

sections.title: Cookies
sections.content: We use essential cookies for language and theme preferences. Optional analytics cookies help us understand site usage. You can manage cookie preferences in Cookie Settings.

sections.title: Third-Party Services
sections.content: We embed Google Maps for location display. Google's own privacy policy applies to map interactions. Photos are sourced from Unsplash and loaded from their CDN.

sections.title: Contact
sections.content: For privacy concerns, contact us at n20186005@gmail.com.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.content: This site provides tourism information about Sameba Cathedral for educational and informational purposes. We are not affiliated with the cathedral administration or the Georgian Orthodox Church.

sections.title: Accuracy
sections.content: Information is sourced from public resources and may not always be current. We recommend verifying opening hours and access details before your visit via Google Maps or official tourism channels.

sections.title: Intellectual Property

sections.title: Limitation of Liability
sections.content: We are not liable for any damages arising from the use of information on this site. Travel and visit decisions are your own responsibility.
